prompt: ABC Inc. has a host of servers, each serving a different purpose. Almost all are critical. Most servers were virtualized with backup virtual components. When one server's memory crashed mid-day, operations were unaffected as it switched to backup virtual memory. What is such a feature called?
Zoe's site crashed under high traffic. She bought two more servers and distributed the load across three servers to maximize efficiency. Which solution did she use?
Which of the following services provides mail and calendar services?
Seth wants an approximate picture of risk likelihood (earthquake unlikely, fire high, intrusion partly controlled by guards) to prioritize mitigation. What kind of risk assessment is Seth conducting?
Which term defines the assessment of risk factors and the relationship between these factors?

Richard backs up daily at 8pm. Fire at 9am destroyed servers. Transfer from alternate site takes 8 hours. Based on last backup, recovery time is 'minus 13 hours'. What term defines this 13-hour period?
Jess wants to implement the standard for societal security in the business continuity management system. Which standard should she implement?
John wants the alternate site to run in parallel to the primary, allowing near-immediate continuity. Which site is best suited?
Katie implemented the RAID level that splits data into blocks and writes evenly across drives but provides no redundancy. This RAID requires a minimum of ___ drives.
Jill wants an analysis to decide if her food truck business would be profitable in monetary terms. Which analysis was she advised to conduct?
XYZ.com (London) lost a hosting server. Admins solved slowness by routing traffic to the alternate server in Mumbai. What term best describes this?
Which technology is used to create a copy of data on one server on another server?
Which team is responsible for the implementation and execution of the disaster recovery plan, including test plans?
Many choose this backup method to protect against site-specific problems; location can be a home office or a disaster-hardened bunker. Which backup method?
Nancy needs RAID with parity stored across multiple drives, reconstruction during downtime, good speed, and not expensive. Which RAID level?
James set aside $20,000 emergency fund for a virtual-connection DR service. What is this emergency fund called?

options.text: Risk Adjusted Return on Risk Adjusted Capital (RARORAC)

prompt: Which is NOT a component of a centralized governance structure for Business Continuity and Risk Management?
Amy mimicked a real-life disaster, testing software, communication, procedures and personnel. Which test?
Which standard on disaster/emergency management & BC programs defines disasters and covers prevention, mitigation, preparedness, response, continuity, and recovery?
Which server category stores host account info and implements security policies in its domain?
Timothy implements a dedicated network for sharing storage resources, separating storage units from servers/user network. He uses a ___.
Jack on Hyper-V wants a snapshot backup instead of full backup of VMs. Which native Hyper-V Manager feature?
Which term refers to the individual skills required to ensure achievement of terminal objectives?
Matt created several logical servers on one physical server. Which technology?
Will sets up a pair of RAID disks so all data written to one is auto-copied to the other. Which RAID level?
Zane finds one server's Active Directory missing; all other domain servers have AD intact. Which restoration process?
Which strategy is NOT part of risk response?
Cameron's software created a copy of data using block level imaging. Which software/method?
Which refers to a central command and control facility responsible for recovery actions, coordinating response/resources, ensuring coherence of operations?
Annabeth (web-hosting, no downtime affordable) opted for live replication at the backup site for immediate switchover. Which backup site?
Fred wants a plan of well-planned actions before, during, and after a catastrophic event. Which plan?
Which technique verifies data is safe, secure, and readable after update, restore, or retrieve?
In which test is the entire BCP implemented?
ABC Bank distributes client data chunks across global centers; a % deviation between actual and targeted data should not exist. Which recovery metric defines this % deviation?
Jonathan classified risks as Very Low/Low/Medium/High/Very High. Which assessment method?
Which term refers to systems that maintain data and access despite malicious/accidental problems with storage servers, networks, clients, and accounts?
Kelly backs up only files created/modified after the last backup. What type?
Which term refers to a set of tasks organizations must continue throughout or resume rapidly after a disruptive incident?
Jack rents a server in the same data center his friend uses; cheaper, provides cooling. Which backup site?
Rachel wants a DR tier that backs up data over a WAN connection. Which tier?
Which method keeps the SYSVOL folder synchronized across multiple servers in the same domain?
Lisa wants to cut cost of many idle dedicated servers, optimize resources, save cooling energy. Which solution?
Rick interconnected individual computers and removed the central server to balance load. Which architecture?
Which term refers to a storage system of HDDs that looks and acts like a physical tape library?
Which is NOT an advantage of colocation facilities?
IT dept copies data changes from original to replica DB server annually (DB updated once/year). Which solution?
Match the RAID components with their functions (1-iii etc.). [配對表PDF未印出選項內容]
Joan's DR test had team members confirm effectiveness, bottlenecks, weaknesses in the plan. Which test?
When information availability increases in PASIS architecture, which feature decreases?
XYZ Inc. transfers critical data to backup site via an FTP server. Which term best describes this?

options.text: Manually copying the data files and transferring them to the remote site

prompt: Which term refers to the ease with which technicians can resolve or replace failing components/systems?
Jack set up BCP training for employees with small roles, covering framework/processes/strategies. What is this training?
Jonah's laptop creates a new backup daily, each taking more space than the previous. Which backup type?
Jonas took measures to avoid/minimize cyclone damage before it hit. Under which classification?
Which DR test is very expensive and disrupts normal business operations?
Gloria's BCP audit stage ascertained whether operational resiliency/continuity was encouraged and if BCP was over/underfunded. Which audit stage?
Emma's scenario training phase: participants informed in advance about training. Which phase?
Which term measures the severity of the impact of a risk on a business?
Alan backs up 3-4 articles daily; backup time = downtime, wants it quick. Which backup suits?
Which term refers to the system's ability to correctly serve the requests it receives?
George identifies risks (phishing, earthquakes, server crash, electrical surges) and must prioritize per an InfoSec risk management standard. Which standard?
Phil's VM crashed during experiment; he had a method taking backups at intervals giving an architectural instance of last saved VM. Which method?
Standard providing a framework to identify/specify all aspects for improving ICT readiness for business continuity. Which standard?
Phoebe's DR test cases need at least 90% to pass for success. What is this scenario known as?
Name of the shared directory in Microsoft Server OS containing copy of commonly shared/replicated public files of a domain?
Using which Windows 10 feature can users preserve system state including system files, installed apps, and settings?
Which cluster provides consistent file system images across servers, allowing simultaneous read/write to a single shared file system?
In PASIS, after implementation critical data was only accessible to authorized employees. Which PASIS feature?
Jonathan used a DR component containing a pictorial representation of all DR processes. Which DR component?
Which term refers to a high-speed data transmission technique providing access to consolidated block-level storage?
Which term refers to storage tech used to capture real-time data changes and facilitate restore points and recovery?
Archie tested the BCP by mobilizing the resources. Which test?
Joanna's software backs up/restores/recovers DB files using database server sessions. Which software?
Which term refers to the recommencement of business functions as systems are gradually made available after a disaster?
Jake's plan identifies all personnel accountable for research, development, and implementation of the DRP. Which plan?
Tom wants centralized storage with free data backup on the server, efficient backup/recovery. Which storage tech?
Where can the VM Management Service be located in the Hyper-V architecture?
Which sub-team assigns research and carries out development/implementation of the DRP throughout the organization?
Fred's site inaccessible; hosting files on server corrupted. Which server must he restore?
Which term refers to a programming pattern where computers are clustered in a distributed and parallel fashion?
Which term refers to a backup taken when the database is offline?
James (PASIS) recovered data from earthquake-destroyed storage nodes. Which PASIS feature allowed recovery?
Company wants a backup method that encrypts data, accessible anytime/anywhere. Which method?
Fred installed generator, mail malware/spam protection, and 8-hr backup to mitigate two risks. Which process did Fred go through?
Which term measures the number of applications/data sets handled by the recovery solution and max data size it can store?
Which components does PASIS architecture integrate?

options.text: Centralized Computing, Data Redundancy and Encoding
Centralized Storage Systems, Deduplication and Mirroring
Decentralized Storage Systems, Data Redundancy and Encoding, and Dynamic Self-Maintenance
Distributed Computing, Data Redundancy and Encoding, and Dynamic Self-Maintenance

prompt: Which term refers to the ability to restore data and applications should data centers/servers/infrastructure get damaged by a disaster?
Betting site: payment gateway down >1hr loses $100k+15%; might lose 25% permanently. Unacceptable, fixed immediately. What term defines this two-hour period?
Jane wants a standard guiding establishment/implementation/maintenance of a formal documented BIA process. Which standard?
Which term refers to a virtualized data storage and processing facility provided as a service?
Which term refers to a dedicated server also used for file storage and sharing?
Martin wants to back up Cisco Router config to an FTP server. Which Cisco IOS CLI command?
Jason notices slow VM data transmission due to poor VMQ handling from NIC restrictions. What should he do?

options.text: Improve the performance of the network storage
Increase the bandwidth of the network connection

prompt: Remy invested $15,000; 15% fail chance rising to 35% after fire-safety inspection. Which method of evaluating risk?
Which term refers to repairing the old site or setting up a new alternate site to resume operations after a disaster?
Lily's archiving method provides tools/software, easy access/cataloging, but recurring cooling & maintenance costs. Which archiving method?
Hospital ER needs zero-minute downtime; secondary server takes over automatically the second primary goes down (coordinated). Which solution?
Which term refers to a client-server architecture merged with layered architecture?
Which term refers to the potential gain from the best alternative forgone when choosing between mutually exclusive alternatives?
Jack updated macOS to a buggy beta; wants to restore to previous OS version. Which MacBook feature?
Which is a feature of mainframe computers?
Which term refers to backing up files modified/changed/added since the last full backup?
Which term refers to evaluation of potential interruptions in a department's working due to failure of their internal systems?
John wants RAID requiring minimum six drives for high fault tolerance and high read/write speed. Which RAID level?
Which term refers to determining the potential impact of disruptive events on an organization's business processes?
Which term refers to third-party services like recovery infrastructure, real-time remote backup, and offsite replication?
Which training is meant for employees who have an important execution role in the BCP, provided framework/processes/strategies?
Mike needs to install a feature in Windows Server 2012 to enable backup/restore of a file server. Which feature via which app?

options.text: Windows Server Backup, Microsoft Management Console

prompt: Which term refers to training milestones that must be achieved to meet the BC training program's aim?
Which DR tier makes use of point in time copies?
Polly estimates magnitude of damage if new product specs leaked (could lose $200k+, > upgrade cost, so upgrades). Which risk factor did she estimate?
Which term refers to the blueprint that calls out the tactics dictating how continuity and recovery from a disaster will be achieved?
Sam (Ubuntu) accidentally deleted official project files but had a backup. Which native Ubuntu option to recover?
Which option must be considered by an organization when conducting BIA?

options.text: Transferring a business activity to a third-party
Establishing alternate processes or creating redundancy/spare capacity in processes
Ceasing or changing a business activity if viable alternatives are available

prompt: Phil's DR tier provided automated recovery of data and applications. Which DR tier?
Which is NOT an objective of business impact analysis?

options.text: Identify RTO and RPO for Each Process
Assess the Impact Caused by a Threat
Identify the Critical Resources Required by the Business
Establish the Recovery Order of the Critical Business Functions

prompt: XYZ messaging app: secondary server can only take load for 4 hours; primary must be up within 4 hours. What term defines this 4-hour period?
Which term refers to bringing a system's application and data back to a previous state in a prescribed, acceptable time frame?
Vulnerability assessment steps: (a) assess impact (b) estimate probability (c) assess resources to mitigate (d) list threats. Correct order?
Oliver's team's proposal leaked to competitor; lost project, morale dropped, other projects delayed for 2 months. Which impact best describes this?
The internal audit of the BCP takes place to ensure conformance to which standard?
Matt (stock brokering, real-time updates) cannot have downtime to back up. Which backup technique?
Jack connected computers/servers into a single unified computing resource, protecting against application/server/software/site failures. Which solution?
Which term refers to a graphical representation of data protection services designed to balance overall cost of data protection against loss?
Which term refers to utilizing a full backup of a file then modifying it with one or more incremental backups?
Roger's test simulated a specific event scenario in which the BCP would be tested. Which test?
Joan's server slowed under traffic spike eating memory; she added extra virtual memory to smooth it. What is such a feature called?
Which cluster uses mounted file systems to read and write data?
Which three components define the three-tier architecture in decentralized computing?

options.text: Client, Application Server, and Database Server

prompt: Mia's computer destroyed in fire; IT used backups to restore a fresh laptop (no OS) to old state, automated, quick. Which data recovery method?
Which term refers to any circumstance with the potential to disrupt or degrade the quality of operations or asset?

Which virtualization method requires software called containers or virtual engines to run?
Which term refers to the delay experienced by a system while it is serving a request?
Matt cannot boot Windows Server 2012 after OS update; boot manager files corrupted. Which command in System Recovery?
Employees create individual backups of same data, wasting storage/bandwidth, risk of leak. What can be done?

options.text: To demonstrate proficiency in management response and crisis conditions
Not threaten the normal business operations of an organization
To diverge from the test storyboard to include unplanned events or circumstances
To ensure the plan is accurate and relevant under adverse circumstances

prompt: When information confidentiality increases in PASIS architecture, what else also increases as a direct result?

Which refers to a moveable operating environment that can be set up at any place on transportable units?

Which statement should a DR coordinator interviewee use to get the post?

options.text: Day-to-day management control over the execution and maintenance of the disaster recovery program
Responsible for the specification and design of all technology-based disaster recovery solutions
Executive authority over the disaster recovery program
Responsible for disaster recovery planning verification and compliance

prompt: Which layer of the Recovery Management Model provides hassle-free recovery and controls data protection technologies like replication and backup?
Remus deleted VM snapshots to free host storage but space wasn't freed. What can he do?

options.text: Shut down or restart the virtual machine system
Delete the child disk of the snapshots present in the host machine

prompt: James's hard drive irreversibly damaged, data unreadable/inaccessible. What type of data loss?

Which term refers to applying strategies to help an organization deal with a sudden and significant undesirable event?

Craig can afford a few days' downtime; pre-installs hardware and pre-configures restoration at backup site to meet RTO. Which backup site?
Which is NOT a component of BIA?
